title: @property
description: Declares a typed CSS custom property — giving it a syntax, an initial value, and inheritance behavior. The browser can now interpolate it, which means custom properties can be used in transitions and animations. Baseline newly available since 2024.
html:
Hover me
No @property
Hover me
Needs @property
challenge: Hover both boxes — the left one jumps instantly while the right should glide smoothly. The browser can't animate --typed-hue without knowing its type. Add an @property rule for --typed-hue with syntax: '<number>' and initial-value: 30.
realWorldUsage: Smooth background-color transitions driven by a hue or lightness variable, animated gradient positions, and custom progress indicators — any animation where the interpolated value lives in a custom property rather than a regular CSS property. @property is also used to define design token types in CSS-native design systems.
